creatorBio: Gabriele Cirulli built 2048 as a weekend project in 2014; it went on to spawn an entire genre of tile matching games.
description: Slide numbered tiles across a 4x4 grid. When two tiles carrying the same number touch, they merge into one tile worth their sum. The board fills up fast, so the real game is deciding which merges to chase and which to leave for later.
howToPlay: Press the arrow keys (or swipe on a touchscreen) to slide every tile on the board in that direction at once. Matching tiles merge into their sum. Reach a 2048 tile before the grid fills up and you run out of moves.

title: Canvas Tetris
creatorBio: Dionysis Zindros wrote this Tetris clone in about 45 minutes as a teaching example for HTML5 canvas basics.
description: A no frills take on the falling block classic. Pieces drop from the top of the board, and clearing a full horizontal line removes it and shifts everything above it down.
howToPlay: Left and right arrows move the current piece sideways, the down arrow moves it down, up rotates it, and space drops it instantly. Clear complete horizontal lines before the stack reaches the top of the board.

title: 0h h1
credit: Q42 (original design), code mirrored by Floris Luiten
creatorBio: Originally designed by Dutch studio Q42, 0h h1 is a binary logic puzzle in the spirit of Sudoku.
description: A binary logic puzzle where every cell is either blue or red. The rules are simple to state and surprisingly tricky to satisfy all at once: no more than two of the same color in a row, each row and column needs an equal split, and no two rows or columns can be identical.
howToPlay: Click a cell to cycle it between empty, blue, and red. Fill the whole grid so every row and column follows the three rules above. The game flags a rule as soon as it's broken and tells you when the puzzle is solved.

title: JS Racer
creatorBio: Jake Gordon built this pseudo 3D racer to demonstrate classic Outrun style rendering tricks in plain JavaScript.
description: A pseudo 3D racing game built the way Sega arcade racers did it in the 80s and 90s: no real 3D models, just clever scaling and curve math drawn on a flat canvas. Traffic, roadside billboards, and hills all react as you drive past.
howToPlay: Left/right arrows or A/D steer, up arrow or W accelerates, down arrow or S brakes. Stay on the road and watch your fastest lap time in the HUD at the top of the screen.

title: Astray
creatorBio: A WebGL maze built with Three.js and Box2D physics by developer wwwtyro.
description: A first person 3D maze with real physics behind it: the ball you control actually rolls and bounces off walls rather than teleporting between grid cells. Find the exit before you lose track of where you've been.
howToPlay: The arrow keys (or the vim style H/J/K/L keys) tilt the maze to roll your ball around. Press I at any time to bring up in game instructions. Find the exit to complete the maze.

title: Asteroids
creatorBio: Doug McInnes wrote this pure JavaScript take on the arcade classic back in 2010.
description: A faithful remake of the 1979 arcade classic in pure JavaScript. Rotate and thrust your ship through an asteroid field, breaking rocks into smaller pieces before they, or the saucers hunting you, catch up.
howToPlay: Left/right arrows rotate your ship, up arrow thrusts forward, and space fires. Destroy asteroids and enemy saucers for points; colliding with either ends your run.

title: Star Battle
creatorBio: Built by gd4Ark for the 2017 Abu Dhabi WorldSkills Competition using vanilla ES6 and Canvas.
description: A top down spaceship shooter where fuel drains constantly, so the pressure never lets up. Shoot enemies for points, grab fuel bottles to stay alive, and watch out for friendly ships, hitting one costs you points instead of earning them.
howToPlay: W/A/S/D moves your ship, space fires. Fuel starts at 15 and drops by 1 every second; collect fuel bottles to refill it, up to a maximum of 30. Hitting enemies scores points, hitting friendlies costs points, and running out of fuel ends the game. P pauses, M mutes.

title: Classic Pool
credit: Chen Shmilovich (music: Kevin MacLeod, CC BY 3.0)
creatorBio: Chen Shmilovich's first JavaScript game project, a full 8 ball pool simulation with AI opponents.
description: A full 8 ball pool simulation with real physics, complete with an AI opponent at three difficulty levels for when you don't have a second player handy.
howToPlay: Move the mouse to aim, hold W to increase shot power and S to decrease it, then left click to take your shot. Press Esc to return to the menu. Play against a friend on the same keyboard or against the built in AI.

title: Bullet Hell
creatorBio: A p5.js bullet hell shooter by selenebun, with both arrow key and WASD control schemes.
description: A dense, screen filling bullet hell shooter. Enemies flood the screen with bullet patterns, and surviving comes down to precise movement and knowing exactly when to spend your limited bombs.
howToPlay: Arrow keys move (or WASD), Z fires (or B), X slows down time for precision dodging (or N), and C uses a screen clearing bomb (or M). Press P to pause.

title: Emoji Minesweeper
creatorBio: Built by Muan (Mu-An Chiou) as a playful, emoji-first take on the classic minesweeper formula.
description: A minesweeper clone that swaps the usual numbers and flags for emoji. Clear every safe tile without triggering one of the hidden bombs, using the numbers that do appear as clues to how many bombs are touching each tile.
howToPlay: Left click a tile to reveal it. Right click a tile to flag it as a suspected bomb. Numbers show how many bombs are in the eight tiles surrounding it. Clear every non-bomb tile to win.

title: Fruit Matcher
creatorBio: Built by mmenavas as a plain HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript project, no frameworks involved.
description: A classic memory matching game with a fruit photography twist. Flip two cards at a time, remember what you saw, and clear the board by finding every matching pair.
howToPlay: Click any face-down card to flip it, then click a second card. If they match, both stay face up. If not, they flip back over. Clear the whole board in as few flips as possible.

title: Connect Four
creatorBio: Built by Bryan Braun as a clean, dependency light implementation of the classic board game.
description: The classic two player drop-and-connect game. Take turns dropping colored pieces down a seven column grid, and be the first to line up four of your own pieces in a row, horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.
howToPlay: Click any column to drop your piece into the lowest open slot in that column. Players alternate turns. First to connect four of their own pieces in a row, in any direction, wins.

title: Checkers
creatorBio: Built by developer gartz as a complete draughts implementation in vanilla JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
description: A full implementation of checkers, also known as draughts, for two players on one keyboard, complete with forced captures, king promotion, and configurable house rules like backward attacks and flying kings.
howToPlay: Click a piece to select it, then click a highlighted square to move it. Jump over an opponent's piece to capture it. Reach the far row to crown a piece as a king. Capture all of your opponent's pieces, or leave them with no legal moves, to win.

title: Hangman
creatorBio: Built by abdoutech19 as a responsive hangman game with GSAP and canvas animations.
description: The classic word guessing game with a category twist: pick from categories like food, sports, and instruments, then guess the hidden word one letter at a time before the drawing is complete.
howToPlay: Pick a category and difficulty, then guess letters one at a time. Correct guesses fill in the word; wrong guesses bring you one step closer to a completed drawing. Guess the whole word before you run out of tries.

title: Word Find
creatorBio: The wordfind.js library and game were built by developer bunkat, with contributions from Lucas-C.
description: A word search puzzle generator and player in one: it builds a fresh grid from a word list, hides them forward, backward, and diagonally, and even offers a solve button if you get stuck.
howToPlay: Find every listed word hidden in the letter grid; words can run in any direction, including backward and diagonally. Click and drag across a word to mark it found. Use Solve Puzzle if you want to see the answers.

title: Reaction Time Tester
creatorBio: Built by himanshusapkota as a small, focused reflex-testing web app.
description: A simple, honest test of how fast your reflexes really are. Wait for the box to turn green, then click as fast as you can. Click too early and you'll have to try again.
howToPlay: Click Start, then wait. After a random delay, the box turns green. Click it as fast as you can. Your reaction time is measured in milliseconds. Clicking before it turns green counts as a false start.

title: Lights Out
creatorBio: Built by Sepand Haghighi as a polished take on the classic Lights Out puzzle.
description: A grid of lights, each one wired to its neighbors. Pressing a light toggles it and every light directly next to it. The goal is to figure out the right sequence of presses to turn every light off.
howToPlay: Click any lit tile to toggle it, along with the tiles directly above, below, left, and right of it. Turn off every light on the board using as few presses as possible. Use the Hint button if you get stuck.

title: 15 Puzzle
creatorBio: Built by Arnis Ritins as a lightweight, dependency-free implementation of the classic 15 puzzle.
description: The sliding tile classic. Fifteen numbered tiles sit in a 4x4 frame with one gap, and the goal is to slide tiles around until they're back in numerical order.
howToPlay: Click any tile adjacent to the empty space to slide it into the gap. Rearrange the tiles into numerical order, left to right, top to bottom, with the empty space ending in the bottom right corner.

title: Snake
creatorBio: Built by Mary Rose Cook as practice material for her game programming workshop at Strange Loop 2014.
description: A minimal, skeletal version of the arcade classic. Guide a growing snake around the board, eating food to grow longer, while avoiding the walls and your own tail.
howToPlay: Use the arrow keys to steer. Eating food makes the snake grow by one block. Running into a wall or into your own tail ends the game.

title: Lunar Lander
creatorBio: Built by Mary Rose Cook as practice material for her game programming workshop at Strange Loop 2014.
description: A physics driven landing challenge inspired by the 1979 arcade classic. Fight gravity and momentum to bring your lander down safely on the landing pad below, rather than crashing into it.
howToPlay: Use the arrow keys to fire your thrusters and rotate the lander. Manage your speed and angle carefully. Touch down gently on the landing pad to succeed; coming in too fast or at too steep an angle ends in a crash.

title: Typing Speed Test
creatorBio: Built by Shreya Agarwal using plain HTML5, CSS, and JavaScript.
description: A straightforward typing benchmark. Type the words shown as accurately and quickly as you can before the timer runs out, choosing your own time limit and difficulty.
howToPlay: Pick a time limit (30 or 60 seconds) and a difficulty, then start typing the displayed words into the box. Your words-per-minute score updates as you go. Try to beat your own best.
